symptom: crampy abdominal pain and vomiting in an older patient with known gallstones
hook: the bowel had ballooned with gas and a stray stone had wandered far from the gallbladder
whatYouSee: Dilated small bowel loops with air outlining the bile ducts and a single stray gallstone sitting low in the abdomen.
whyItMatters: A large gallstone has eroded through into the gut and lodged in the small bowel causing mechanical obstruction and it mostly strikes older patients and is easy to miss.
treatment: Treatment is surgical removal of the obstructing stone with later attention to the gallbladder fistula.
takeaway: Bowel obstruction with air in the bile ducts and a stray stone is gallstone ileus.

condition.symptom: crampy abdominal pain and vomiting in an older patient with known gallstones
condition.hook: the bowel had ballooned with gas and a stray stone had wandered far from the gallbladder
condition.keyFindings: the Rigler triad of dilated gas-filled small bowel loops plus gas in the biliary tree and an ectopic gallstone lodged in the bowel
condition.whatYouSee: Dilated small bowel loops with air outlining the bile ducts and a single stray gallstone sitting low in the abdomen.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with crampy abdominal pain and vomiting in an older patient with known gallstones.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the bowel had ballooned with gas and a stray stone had wandered far from the gallbladder.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Gallstone ileus

👀 What you see:
Dilated small bowel loops with air outlining the bile ducts and a single stray gallstone sitting low in the abdomen.

🦴 Why it matters:
A large gallstone has eroded through into the gut and lodged in the small bowel causing mechanical obstruction and it mostly strikes older patients and is easy to miss.

📝 Takeaway:
Bowel obstruction with air in the bile ducts and a stray stone is gallstone ileus.
